title: Write table-driven invalid percentage tests
scenario: Invalid cases deserve precise tests too. Table-driven tests make it clear which inputs should fail.
instructions: Keep the valid table-driven test from the starter. Add a second test that loops over invalid values and checks PercentageError::OutOfRange.

hints.body: Do not replace accepts_valid_percentages; add rejects_invalid_percentages in the same tests module.
Use an invalid_values table such as [101, 150, 1_000] and assert each conversion returns Err(PercentageError::OutOfRange).
One complete solution for Write table-driven invalid percentage tests is shown below.

completionExplanation: Invalid Percentage boundaries are now covered by an additive table-driven test without losing the valid-case table.
